channel.title: clawfeedradar
channel.description: Personal feed radar powered by clawsqlite.

item.title: Power-washing, pool-cleaning and mowing: Why millions are playing games about mundane jobs
item.description: PowerWash Simulator 2 has been nominated for two Bafta Games Awards - but why have mundane job games become so popular?

item.title: Claude Code users hitting usage limits 'way faster than expected'
item.description: Anthropic, the company behind the AI coding assistant, said it was fixing a problem blocking users.

item.title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
item.description: It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

item.title: Tech CEOs suddenly love blaming AI for mass job cuts. Why?
item.description: More tech leaders are pointing to job cuts caused by AI tools - and a need for more investment cash.

item.title: Tech Life
item.description: Attribution science is becoming an increasingly important tool in climate lawsuits.

item.title: Fewer UK adults posting on social media, Ofcom finds
item.description: Some experts believe it highlights a social media shift as platforms boost short video.

item.title: Why Chinese tech companies are racing to set up in Hong Kong
item.description: Mainland firms are using the territory to test products and as a springboard for global expansion.

item.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
item.description: Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

item.title: 'We're having a moment' - fear and denial in Silicon Valley over social media addiction trial
item.description: The landmark decision in an LA court may go beyond immediate impacts on defendants Meta and YouTube.

item.title: Social media firms must better enforce Australia under-16 ban, watchdog says
item.description: Regulator eSafety says it has concerns about how Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are complying with the ban.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to be worth $1 trillion with planned public listing
item.description: The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

item.title: Tech Life
item.description: Humans are returning to the Moon - hear about it on the BBC’s space podcast, 13 Minutes
